meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Template to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the no-portfolio hurdle. Learn to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to build high-impact, professional SaaS projects fast.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is solved by shipping high-value, functional code, not theory. Use the Antigravity template to scaffold production-ready architecture, then leverage Claude Code to accelerate feature implementation. This combination transforms raw ideas into a professional, deployable SaaS project that proves your engineering capability to potential employers.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and the silence of an empty GitHub profile feels louder than any rejection email. You have the skills, but the industry demands proof—a tangible, breathing application that demonstrates you can ship, not just study. The gap between a tutorial-follower and a software engineer is built on the architecture you choose to defend.

Today, we bridge that gap. We are using the Antigravity project template, a high-octane scaffolding tool designed to eliminate boilerplate, paired with the raw velocity of Claude Code. You aren't just building a project; you are constructing a reputation. Let's turn that empty repository into a flagship piece of your professional portfolio.

steps.title: Scaffold the Production Environment
steps.description: Initialize your project using the Antigravity template to establish a professional-grade folder structure, CI/CD pipelines, and environment management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a full-stack SaaS boilerplate with Next.js 14, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase integration, focusing on modular architecture and clean patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize Antigravity template with Next.js and Supabase'

steps.title: Define the Core Data Schema
steps.description: Establish a robust database schema that reflects real-world constraints, such as user roles and subscription tiers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a SaaS platform including users, profiles, and subscription plans with proper foreign key constraints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create migration scripts for users and subscription tables'

steps.title: Implement Authentication Flows
steps.description: Secure your application by integrating industry-standard auth providers, ensuring user data privacy from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Supabase Auth with email verification and protected routes in the Next.js app router.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement protected middleware and auth redirects'

steps.title: Accelerate Feature Logic with Claude
steps.description: Use Claude Code to iterate on business logic, focusing on edge cases that show senior-level thinking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an API route for handling Stripe webhooks to manage subscription lifecycle events safely.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Add Stripe webhook handler with secure signature verification'

steps.title: Optimize for Production Deployment
steps.description: Finalize your project by configuring environmental variables and production build settings.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review current implementation for performance bottlenecks and prepare configuration for Vercel deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Audit code for production best practices and security vulnerabilities'

pro_tips: Always commit your progress with granular, atomic messages to show a history of intentional engineering.
Include a 'Design Decisions' document in your repo; showing WHY you chose a tool is more important than the tool itself.
Implement automated testing early; a project without tests is just a prototype, not a portfolio piece.
Focus on one 'hero' feature that solves a specific, complex problem rather than cloning generic SaaS dashboards.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write it; clean documentation is the hallmark of a senior developer.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, write a 'Case Study' blog post explaining the technical challenges you overcame, then link it in your GitHub README to attract recruiters.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your code sit in private folders. It is time to ship, scale, and show the world what you are capable of building. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
